name: Deployables Self-Supporting
text: Deployables shall, where practicable, be designed so that they are self supporting when placed in any orientation relative to gravity, while in either the stowed or deployed configuration.

name: Ground Test Motive Force
text: Deployables shall be designed with sufficient motive force to permit full operation during ground testing without depending upon the assistance of gravity.

name: Deployable Redundancy
text: Deployables shall utilize redundancy, where practicable, in the design to improve the reliability of deployment.

name: Redundant Springs Consideration
text: Techniques such as redundant springs or the use of a backup deployment device shall be considered to satisfy the redundancy requirement.

name: CG Alignment for Rotating Deployables
text: Where a device rotates after deployment, the design shall, where practicable, be such that the center of gravity is coincident with the axis of rotation.

name: Controlled or Restrained Deployment
text: For all deployables, the deployment motion shall, where practicable, be controlled or restrained for the full range of travel.

name: Kick-Off Springs
text: Kick-off springs or other suitable devices shall, where practicable, be used to initiate deployment motion.

name: Kick-Off Spring Stroke
text: Kick-off springs shall have a stroke long enough to ensure complete disengagement of the deployable from its retention mechanism.

name: Field Joints for Disassembly
text: Field joints shall be provided, where practicable, to permit disassembly of deployables and rotating or translating elements.

name: Sequential Pyro Events
text: Deployables requiring discrete sequential motions initiated by separate pyrotechnic events shall have each event separately ground commanded or have a backup ground command.

name: Cable Guidance
text: Where cables are used in the deployment mechanism, they shall be adequately guided and supported by fairleads.

name: Pulley Guards
text: All pulleys shall utilize pulley guards which extend to the tangency points of the cable.

name: No Dependency Between Deployables
text: Where two or more deployables are used on a vehicle, the deployment of one shall not be dependent upon the successful deployment of the other, unless unavoidable.

name: Redundant Release for Mission-Critical Sequential Deployables
text: Where sequential dependency cannot be avoided and the obstructed deployable is mission critical, a fully redundant release device and backup deployment mechanism shall be provided.

name: Positive Retention in Stowed and Deployed Positions
text: Positive retention provisions shall be provided for deployables in the stowed and deployed positions.

name: Consider Deflections in Attachment Design
text: The effects of deflections such as centrifugal forces or differential thermal growth shall be considered in the design of deployable attachments.

name: Avoid Binding-Prone Devices
text: Devices subject to binding due to misalignment, adverse tolerances, or contamination shall not be used.

name: Avoid Slip Joints
text: Slip joints shall be avoided, where practicable.

name: Use Self-Aligning Features
text: Self-aligning features such as self-aligning bearings and rod ends shall be used, where practicable, to preclude binding.

name: No Continuous Hinges for Large Panels
text: Continuous hinges such as piano hinges shall not be used for large deployable panels.

name: Avoid Ejection-Type Release Mechanisms
text: Release mechanisms which permit ejection of parts away from the vehicle shall be avoided, where practicable.

name: Minimize Latch Resistance Peaking
text: The design of latching devices shall be such that peaking of resistance near the end of travel is minimized.

name: Avoid Leaf Spring Latches
text: Leaf spring latches which become the primary element for reacting deployment or rebound loads shall be avoided, where practicable.

name: Avoid Permanent Magnet Catches
text: Catches using a permanent magnet as the holding element shall be avoided, where practicable.

name: Pin Pullers in Double Shear
text: Where pin pullers are used, they shall be designed to be in double shear.

name: Pin Puller Misalignment Loads
text: The design, installation, and checkout procedures for pin pullers shall ensure that loads due to misalignment are within design limits.

name: Pin Puller Retraction Margin
text: A minimum retraction force margin of safety of 100 percent shall be maintained for all nonexplosive pin pullers.

name: Pin Puller Stroke Requirement
text: Pin pullers shall retract at least 2 millimeters beyond the point of release under worst case tolerances and environmental conditions.

name: Pyrotechnic Devices per DOD-EL83578
text: Cartridge actuated device designs and their application shall comply with DOD-EL83578.

name: Redundant Pyrotechnic Release Devices
text: Redundant pyrotechnic release devices shall be used, where practicable.

name: Test Fixture Compatibility
text: Fixtures used to assist in fabrication and testing shall be designed in conjunction with the deployable device to ensure compatibility.

name: Minimize Influence of Supplemental Supports
text: Supplemental supports shall be designed so that their influence on the operation of the mechanism is minimized.

name: Test Fixture Measurement Provisions
text: Test fixtures shall incorporate provisions to measure torque vs angle and time vs angle, or equivalent linear measurements.

name: Test Fixture Safety Interlocks
text: Where practicable, test fixtures shall have interlocks or other safety features incorporated to preclude damage to the moving mechanical assembly or deployable in the event of testing malfunctions.

name: Tethers and Backup Supports
text: Tethers and supports shall be incorporated as backup features to preclude damage in the event of failure.

name: Use Self-Aligning Bearings
text: For deployables, hinges, and linkages, self-aligning bearings shall be used, where practicable, to preclude binding due to misalignments.

name: Bearings Not for Electrical Current
text: Bearings shall not be used for ground current return paths or to carry electrical current.

name: Use Corrosion-Resistant Steel for Ferrous Bearings
text: All ferrous material bearings shall employ, where practicable, a corrosion-resistant steel that is in accordance with QQ-S-763.

name: Rolling Element Bearing Material
text: Rolling element bearings shall, where practicable, be of 440C stainless steel; however, 52100 or M50 steels may be employed providing they are suitably protected from corrosion.

name: Rolling Element Bearing Hardness
text: Rolling element bearings shall have a minimum hardness of Rockwell C58.

name: CEVM Material for Critical Bearings
text: Ball bearings used in applications where low torque ripple is required or where fatigue life is critical shall, where practicable, be fabricated of consumable electrode vacuum melted (CEVM) material.

name: ABEC 7 Bearings for Critical Applications
text: Bearings used in critical applications such as reaction wheels, control moment gyros, gimbals, de-spin mechanisms, and pointing devices shall meet ABEC 7, 7P or 7T tolerance or better.

name: Nonstandard Bearings Equivalent to ABEC 7
text: Nonstandard bearings or thin sectioned bearings where AFBMA tolerances do not apply shall have the manufacturer's precision level most nearly equivalent to ABEC 7.

name: ABEC 5 for Noncritical Deployables
text: Bearings used in noncritical applications such as one time use deployables shall, where practicable, meet ABEC 5 or better.

name: Stainless Bearings per MIL-B-81793
text: Stainless steel bearings shall, where practicable, also be in accordance with MIL-B-81793.

name: Bearing Design Based on Combined Loads
text: Ball bearing selection, mounting, and preloading shall be based on maximum combined axial, radial, and moment loads sustained during all operational modes.

name: Bearing Design Must Consider Stiffness
text: Stiffness requirements shall be considered in bearing selection, mounting, and preloading.

name: Consider Temperature Effects on Bearings
text: Effects of temperature, temperature gradients, fits, tolerances, and initial preload on torque, stiffness, and life shall be considered.

name: Bearing Lubrication Consideration
text: Lubrication shall be considered in bearing selection, mounting, and preloading.

name: Bearing Wear Consideration
text: Wear shall be considered in bearing selection, mounting, and preloading.

name: Smoothness of Operation
text: Smoothness of operation, including torque ripple, shall be considered in bearing design.

name: Friction Torque Consideration
text: Friction torques, considering breakaway and running, in the installed state shall be considered.

name: Bearing Design Substantiation
text: The design of each ball bearing installation shall be substantiated by analysis and either development tests or previous usage.

name: Quiet Operation Bearing Hertzian Stress Limit
text: For noncritical applications, such as most deployables, if nonquiet running is acceptable, the mean Hertzian contact stress shall not exceed 2758 megapascals (400,000 psi) when subjected to the yield load.

name: Lubrication Required for All Contacting Surfaces
text: Lubrication shall be provided by greases, liquids, solid film lubricants, or combinations thereof for all contacting surfaces having relative motion.

name: Lubricant Selection Criteria
text: The selection of lubricants for moving mechanical assemblies shall be based on coefficient of friction, vacuum behavior, depletion, temperature limits, viscosity, outgassing, corrosion protection, polymerization, cleanliness, run-in requirements, and compatibility.

name: Lubricant Shall Not Cause Detrimental Effects
text: The lubricant chosen shall not cause detrimental effects on the moving mechanical assembly during or after operation at ambient conditions.

name: Vent Paths for Liquid Lubricants
text: The vent paths from all liquid lubricants shall be designed such that the outgassing products do not impinge directly on critical surfaces.

name: Lubricant Selection Based on Development Tests
text: The selection of the lubricant for use in the critical moving mechanical assembly shall be based upon development tests demonstrating adequate lubrication under all specified operating conditions over the design lifetime.

name: Lubricant and Process Documentation
text: The lubricant selected, and the processes used in cleaning parts and applying the lubricant shall be delineated.

name: Corrosion Inhibitors for M50 and 52100
text: Lubricants used with M50 or 52100 gear materials shall include corrosion inhibitors which prevent corrosion when the assembly is humidity tested in accordance with MIL-STD-1540.

name: Maximize Lubricant Film Thickness
text: In rolling element bearings, the lubricant film thickness shall be maximized consistent with lubricant type, operating conditions, and bearing parameters.

name: Boundary Lubrication Requirements
text: If bearings must be operated in the boundary lubrication regime, a boundary lubricant with good anti-wear characteristics shall be used.

name: Reservoir Attachment for Lubricant Flow
text: Where bearing lubricant reservoirs are used, the reservoir shall be attached, where practicable, to an area of relatively high temperature to enhance molecular and surface flow into the bearing.

name: Dry Film Lubricant Application Process
text: Application of dry film lubricants shall be by an appropriate process that provides a predictable, uniform, and repeatable lubricant film.

name: Corrosion Resistant Materials for Dry Film Bearings
text: Corrosion resistant materials shall be used in bearings employing dry film lubricants.

name: Protect MoS2 from Humidity
text: Consideration shall be given to protection of molybdenum disulfide dry film lubricants from adverse effects due to exposure to atmospheric humidity.

name: Hard Coating Application Process
text: Hard coatings shall be applied by an appropriate process such as ion implantation or chemical vapor deposition that assures the coating will not flake off under maximum stress.

name: Hydraulic Fluid Conformance
text: Fluids used in hydraulic systems shall be in conformance with MIL-H-6083 or MIL-H-5605.

name: Brush and Slip Ring Material Selection
text: Brush and slip ring materials shall be selected to minimize wear, electrical noise, and contamination.

name: Electrical Parts Shall Meet MIL-STD-1547
text: Electrical and electronic parts used in moving mechanical assemblies shall meet the requirements of MIL-STD-1547.

name: Motor Selection Based on Performance Requirements
text: Electric motors shall be selected based on torque, speed, power, efficiency, thermal characteristics, and environmental requirements.

name: Motor Thermal Protection
text: Motors shall be protected from overheating by design features, thermal switches, or current limiting devices.

name: Stepper Motor Detent Torque Minimization
text: Stepper motors shall be selected and designed to minimize detent torque where smooth motion is required.

name: Stepper Motor Resonance Avoidance
text: Stepper motor drive systems shall be designed to avoid resonant frequencies that could cause loss of synchronism.

name: Torque Motor Continuous Stall Operation
text: Torque motors shall be capable of continuous stall operation without exceeding allowable temperature limits.

name: Test and Inspection Facility Capability
text: The manufacturer shall ensure that test and inspection facilities of sufficient accuracy, quality, and quantity are established and maintained to permit performance of required inspections.

name: Control of Parts and Materials Prior to Assembly
text: To ensure that a reliable moving mechanical assembly is fabricated, all parts and materials shall be adequately controlled and inspected prior to assembly.

name: Control of Tools, Processes, Parts, and Materials During Fabrication
text: During fabrication, the tools and processes, as well as parts and materials, shall be adequately controlled and inspected to ensure compliance with the approved manufacturing processes and controls.

name: Accreditation Records Maintenance
text: Records documenting the accreditation status of the moving mechanical assemblies shall be maintained following assignment of serial numbers.

name: Serial Number Traceability Records
text: Each moving mechanical assembly shall have inspection records and test records maintained by serial number to provide trace-ability from system usage to production lot data for the device.

name: Complete Records Availability
text: Complete records shall be maintained and shall be available for review during the service life of the system.

name: Record Content Requirements
text: The records shall indicate all relevant test data, all rework or modifications, and all installation and removals for whatever reason.

name: In-Process Manufacturing Screens
text: Each moving mechanical assembly and each critical subassembly shall be subjected to in-process manufacturing and assembly screens to ensure compliance with the specified requirements to the extent practicable.

name: Verification of Process Controls and Configuration
text: Compliance with the documented process controls, documented screening requirements, required hardware configuration, and general workmanship requirements shall be verified.

name: Visual Inspection at Each Assembly Level
text: At each level of assembly, each completed unit shall be subjected to visual inspection to ensure that it is free of obvious defects and is within specified physical limits.

name: Reject Nonconforming Material
text: Nonconforming material, components, or assemblies that do not meet the established tolerance limits set for the acceptance limits in the in-process screens shall be rejected for use.

name: No Reassignment of Lot-Controlled Units
text: Reassignment of units that were lot controlled to a different lot shall not be made.

name: Scrap Nonconforming Material Not Meeting Rework Criteria
text: Nonconforming material or assembled units that do not satisfy these rework criteria shall be considered scrap.

name: Inspection of Castings, Welds, Composite Parts, and Stressed Surfaces
text: As a minimum, all castings, fusion welds, fibre composite or honeycomb parts and surfaces of stressed parts shall be inspected as follows:

name: Radiographic Inspection of Castings and Welds
text: All castings and all fusion welds shall be inspected for the presence of internal defects by radiographic methods in accordance with MIL-STD-453.

name: Internal Inspection of Composite Structures
text: Plastic composite structural elements shall be inspected for the presence of internal defects by an appropriate test method.

name: Surface Inspection of Stressed Parts
text: The surfaces of stressed parts shall be inspected in accordance with MIL-X-6868, MIL-STD-6866, or other appropriate inspection methods.

name: Nital Etch Inspection for High-Strength Steel
text: Heat-treated steel parts with tensile strength above 1,790 MPa that require a grinding operation after final heat treatment shall be inspected by Nital etch on all surfaces subject to grinding.

name: Complete Inspection of Critical or Single-Point-Failure Parts
text: All parts used in critical moving mechanical assemblies or parts comprising single point failures shall be completely inspected to ensure that no defects exist which could lead to degraded performance or failure.

name: Assembly Dimensional and Physical Inspection
text: The dimensions, weight, finish, identification markings, and cleanliness of each moving mechanical assembly shall be inspected, as appropriate, prior to acceptance testing and prior to installation on the vehicle.

name: Thread Engagement Requirements
text: Inspection of the assembly shall be made to ensure that a minimum thread engagement of five full threads exists for all threaded attachments; or where through bolts are used, that a minimum of two full threads protrude beyond the end of the nut.

name: Fastener Torque Inspection
text: Fasteners shall be inspected for compliance with required torque levels.

name: Concurrent Assembly Inspections
text: These inspections shall be made concurrently with the assembly of the parts.

name: Pin Puller Travel Verification
text: Pin pullers shall be inspected at the vehicle test level to verify adequate travel and travel margin.

name: Pre- and Post-Environmental Test Inspections
text: All moving mechanical assemblies shall be inspected before and after exposure to environmental tests at the component test level, at the vehicle test level, and at the launch site.

name: Surface Damage Rejection Criteria
text: If surface damage is sufficient to affect the intended function of the assembly, or the performance is degraded, the moving mechanical assembly shall be rejected.

name: Reject Rust or Corrosion
text: Any parts exhibiting rust or corrosion shall be rejected.

name: Fastener Looseness Inspection
text: Fasteners shall be visually inspected for looseness.

name: Torque Check Before and After Environmental Tests
text: The torque shall be checked on pre-selected fasteners before and after exposure to each environmental test condition.

name: Establish Lubricant Processing Procedures
text: Lubricant processing procedures shall be established to control the uniformity, repeatability, cleanliness, accountability, and amount of lubricant in the moving mechanical assemblies.

name: Removal of Shipping Lubricants
text: Lubricant processing procedures shall delineate the processes of removal of bearing shipping lubricants.

name: Analyze Shipping Lubricant Compatibility
text: Shipping lubricants shall be analyzed for compatibility with the applied lubricant.

name: Replace Porous Retainers Containing Shipping Lubricants
text: Porous ball bearing retainers and lubricant reservoirs which have shipping lubricants in them shall be replaced before processing with the flight lubricant.

name: Wetability Testing of Bearings
text: Each bearing to be processed with a liquid lubricant shall be tested for wetability prior to application of the flight lubricant.

name: Approval of Wetability Test Method
text: The wetability test method shall be approved by the contracting officer.

name: Use Developmental Testing to Validate New Designs
text: Developmental testing shall be used to validate new design concepts for critical components and assemblies as a prelude to qualification testing.

name: Development Testing Must Ensure Minimal Failures During Qualification
text: The nature and extent of developmental testing on components, subsystems, and complete moving mechanical assemblies shall be sufficient to ensure that qualification testing of new designs will produce minimal failures which would then require redesign.

name: Development Testing Shall Use MIL-STD-1540 as a Guide
text: Developmental testing shall be conducted using MIL-STD-1540 as a guide.

name: Bearing Installation Design Substantiation
text: Tests to substantiate the design of each bearing installation with respect to materials, stresses, stiffness, fatigue life, preload, and possible binding under normal, as well as the most severe combined loading conditions, and the expected environmental conditions shall be conducted.

name: Bearing Reuse Wear and Lubrication Characterization
text: For bearings that are reused, tests to characterize the wear and lubrication changes resulting from bearing reuse, and the effects these changes have on torque margin, shall be conducted.

name: Temperature Gradient Effects on Bearing Preload
text: Where practicable, tests to substantiate the effects of temperature gradients on bearing preload and stiffness shall be conducted.

name: Lubricant System Adequacy Testing
text: For critical moving mechanical assemblies, tests of the lubricant that demonstrate the ability of the lubricant system to provide adequate lubrication under all specified operating conditions over the design lifetime shall be conducted.

name: Friction Welding Verification Tests
text: Where contact pressure is sufficiently high, such that friction welding might be possible, design and process verification tests shall be conducted at the piece part or subassembly level to verify the selection of materials and lubricants in the proposed application.

name: Brush and Commutator Lubrication and Wear Testing
text: For brushes and commutator or slip ring assemblies, tests shall demonstrate that the lubricant is not significantly deteriorated or driven from the areas requiring lubrication by adverse thermal, gravity forces, or other conditions; and that wear rates, wear debris, electrical noise level, friction torque, and lubricant loss are compatible with design requirements.

name: Deployable Driving Capability and Load Testing
text: For deployable devices, minimum available driving capability and maximum load determination shall be verified through an appropriate test program; each element of resistance shall be characterized in this test program.

name: Motor Torque vs Current Characterization
text: For moving mechanical assemblies driven by electrical motors, a torque versus current relationship for each motor under minimum, maximum, and ambient thermal and vacuum conditions shall be established.

name: Eddy Current Damper Life Testing
text: Eddy current dampers shall be tested for repeatability of damping rate and torque values for twice its expected operational life cycle.

name: Eddy Current Damper Temperature Testing
text: The eddy current damper shall be tested at temperature extremes to determine its damping rate over the temperature range.

name: First Assembly Inspection Requirement
text: An inspection of the first of each of the moving mechanical assemblies produced with production equipment and procedures shall be conducted at a time and location acceptable to the contracting officer.

name: No Discrepancies Among Equipment, Tooling, Drawings, and Records
text: There shall be no discrepancies among the equipment, the fabrication tooling used, the released drawings, the test data, the inspection records, and the specification requirements.

name: First Assembly Approval Validity
text: First assembly approval is valid only on the contract or purchase order under which it is granted, unless extended by the contracting officer to other contracts or purchase orders.

name: Verify Configuration and Workmanship Before Acceptance Testing
text: The configuration and workmanship of the completed hardware shall be verified by inspection prior to the start of acceptance testing.

name: Conduct Acceptance Tests per MIL-STD-1540
text: Acceptance tests shall be conducted on each moving mechanical assembly in accordance with the acceptance test requirements of MIL-STD-1540.

name: Acceptance Tests Shall Include Run-In, Functional, and Environmental Tests
text: The acceptance tests shall incorporate the run-in, functional, and environmental test requirements stated herein.

name: Acceptance Tests Must Detect Workmanship Defects
text: The acceptance tests shall be structured to detect workmanship defects that could affect operational performance.

name: Proof Testing of Composite or Honeycomb Components
text: Components fabricated from fiber composite material or of honeycomb construction shall be subjected to acceptance proof testing.

name: Acceptance Testing with Deployable Device Attached
text: The acceptance testing of moving mechanical assemblies that are part of a deployable device shall, where practicable, be conducted with the moving mechanical assembly attached to the deployable device.

name: Dummy Load Requirements for Acceptance Testing
text: The dummy loads shall provide a reasonable representation of the dynamic characteristics (such as inertia, stiffness, free play, and natural frequencies) of the actual driven member.

name: Account for Dummy Load Friction Effects
text: The effect of friction due to the additional weight of the dummy load on the drive unit shall be accounted for in evaluating the test results.

name: Torque and Angle Measurement Requirements
text: Appropriate quantitative measurements such as torque vs angle measurements and time vs angle measurements, or equivalent linear measurements for linear devices, shall be made during run-in, functional, and environmental acceptance testing of all moving mechanical assemblies.

name: Torque vs Angle Hysteresis for Spring-Driven Devices
text: For spring driven devices, torque vs angle measurements shall be made during restowing as well as during deployment in order to generate a torque vs angle hysteresis curve to determine torque margin.

name: Force Margin Measurements for Linear Devices
text: For linear devices, equivalent linear measurements shall be made to determine force margin.

name: Measurements Required at Ambient and Hot/Cold Acceptance Temperatures
text: As a minimum, these measurements shall be made under ambient and acceptance level hot and cold temperatures.

name: Redundant Modes Must Be Tested
text: Moving mechanical assemblies that contain redundancy in their design shall, where practicable, demonstrate performance to their requirements in each redundant mode of operation.

name: Functional Operation and Alignment Before and After Environmental Tests
text: Functional operation and alignment of each moving mechanical assembly, including instrumentation that is an integral part of the assembly, shall be tested and checked, where practicable, before and after exposure to each environmental acceptance test.

name: Swaged Ends Must Be Proof Tested
text: All swaged ends on rods or cables shall be 100 percent tested with a load equal to twice the limit load.

name: Run-In Test Required for Each Moving Mechanical Assembly
text: After initial functional testing, a run-in test shall be performed on each moving mechanical assembly before it is subjected to further acceptance testing, unless it can be shown that this procedure would be detrimental to performance and would result in reduced reliability.

name: Minimum Run-In Duration (Hours or Cycles)
text: The run-in test shall be conducted for a minimum of 50 hours except for items where the number of cycles of operation, rather than hours of operation, is a more appropriate measure of the capability to perform in a consistent and controlled manner.

name: Minimum Run-In Cycles Requirement
text: For these units, the run-in test shall be for at least 15 cycles or 5 percent of the total expected service life cycles, whichever is greater.

name: Run-In Test Conditions Must Be Representative
text: The run-in test conditions should be representative of the operational loads, speed, and environment; however, operation of the assembly at ambient conditions may be conducted if the test objectives can be met and the ambient environment will not degrade reliability or cause unacceptable changes to occur within the equipment such as the generation of excessive debris.

name: Periodic Measurements During Run-In
text: During the run-in test, sufficient periodic measurements shall be made to indicate what conditions may be changing with time and what wear rate characteristics exist.

name: Run-In Test Plan Approval
text: Test procedures, test time, and criteria for performance adequacy shall be in accordance with a test plan that has been approved by the contracting officer.

name: Post Run-In Inspection of Solid Film Lubricated Gear Trains
text: All gear trains using solid film lubricants shall, where practicable, be inspected and cleaned following the run-in test.

name: Functional and Environmental Tests Required
text: Each moving mechanical assembly shall be subjected to functional and environmental tests that are in accordance with the applicable acceptance test requirements of MIL-STD-1540.

name: Functional Tests Must Demonstrate Performance Requirements
text: Functional tests shall be structured to demonstrate that the moving mechanical assembly is capable of operating in such a manner that all performance requirements are satisfied.

name: Functional Tests Required Before and After Environmental Exposure
text: Functional tests are required before and after exposure to environmental test conditions in order to establish whether damage or degradation in performance has occurred.

name: Functional Tests Must Be Comprehensive
text: These tests shall be sufficiently comprehensive and shall include sufficient measurements to determine whether performance specifications are met.

name: Functional Tests Must Be Conducted in Relevant Environments
text: Where a device is designed to operate in extreme heat or cold or in some other environmental extreme, and a functional test at ambient conditions would not be significant, the functional tests shall be conducted in the appropriate environment that would demonstrate performance.

name: Test Assemblies in Launch or On-Orbit Configuration
text: The moving mechanical assemblies shall be tested in their launch or in their on-orbit configurations corresponding to the environment being simulated.

name: Assemblies Must Be Passive or Operating as in Launch/Orbit
text: They shall be passive or operating corresponding to their state during the launch or on-orbit operational phases.

name: Torque and Angle Measurements During Thermal Vacuum Testing
text: Fully assembled deployables shall, where practicable, have torque vs angle and time vs angle measurements, or equivalent linear measurements for linear devices, made during thermal vacuum testing.

name: Harmonic Drive Dedoidal Inspection
text: After a harmonic drive is assembled into the parent assembly, it shall be inspected for the dedoidal condition.

name: Hot/Cold Box Testing When Vacuum Testing Is Impractical
text: When operation of a deployable in a vacuum chamber or measurements in a vacuum chamber are impractical, the fully assembled deployable shall have deployment tests, including torque vs angle and time vs angle measurements, or equivalent linear measurements for linear devices, made in a hot or cold box.

name: Localized Hot/Cold Box Testing for Large Deployables
text: When the fully assembled deployable is so large that it cannot be completely enclosed within a hot or cold box, the moving mechanical assemblies themselves may be separately enclosed within several smaller hot or cold boxes which surround only the moving mechanical assembly portion of the deployable device.

name: Test Plan Approval for Functional and Environmental Tests
text: Test procedures, test time, and criteria for performance adequacy shall be as approved by the contracting officer.

name: No Disassembly or Repair Allowed During Testing Without Approval
text: No disassembly, adjustments, or repair shall be made on moving mechanical assemblies during functional and environmental acceptance testing or after completion of testing unless approved by the contracting officer.

name: Conduct Qualification Tests per MIL-STD-1540
text: The qualification tests of the moving mechanical assemblies shall be conducted in accordance with the qualification test requirements of MIL-STD-1540.

name: Qualification Tests Must Include Design Life, Functional, and Environmental Tests
text: The qualification tests shall incorporate the design life verification test, functional test, and environmental test requirements stated herein.

name: Dedicated Qualification Items Required for Multi-Use Assemblies
text: Moving mechanical assemblies which are required to operate more than once during their on-orbit life shall be tested using dedicated qualification items.

name: Flight Use of Qualification Article Requires Approval
text: Any flight use of a qualification article shall have prior approval by the contracting officer.

name: Qualification Tests Must Demonstrate Design Adequacy and Margins
text: The qualification tests shall be structured to demonstrate the design adequacy and design margins.

name: Acceptance Test Must Precede Qualification Test
text: An acceptance test shall precede the qualification tests.

name: Torque and Angle Measurements Required During Qualification
text: Torque vs angle measurements and time vs angle measurements, or equivalent linear measurements for linear devices, shall be made during qualification testing of all moving mechanical assemblies.

name: Contracting Officer Approval Required for Alternate Qualification Evidence
text: Compliance with all or portions of the qualification requirements based upon tests of similar items or upon equivalent vehicle operational performance shall require approval of the contracting officer.

name: Qualification Test Configurations Must Match Acceptance Tests
text: The general test measurements and test configurations used for qualification tests shall be similar to those used for acceptance tests.

name: Redundant Modes Must Be Tested During Qualification
text: Moving mechanical assemblies that contain redundancy in their design shall, where practicable, demonstrate performance to their requirements in each redundant mode of operation during the qualification test.

name: Functional Operation and Alignment Before and After Qualification Tests
text: Functional operation and alignment of each moving mechanical assembly, including instrumentation that is an integral part of the assembly, shall, where practicable, be tested and checked, before and after exposure to each qualification test.

name: Design Life Verification Test Required
text: One or more items of each of the moving mechanical assemblies, produced with production equipment and procedures, shall be subjected to a design life verification test.

name: Simulate Worst Predicted Operational Environments
text: These tests shall be conducted to simulate operational use within the range of the worst predicted operational environments.

name: Demonstrate Full Operational Life Cycle Capability
text: The items shall be subjected to tests which demonstrate the capability to perform the full operational life cycle.

name: Simulate Operational Masses, Loads, and Stiffness
text: Masses, loads, and stiffness of support structure shall be actual or simulated operational values, and any interfacing equipment such as thermal heaters, cabling, or hoses shall be in place so that operational conditions are simulated.

name: Design Life Test Plan Approval
text: The test and criteria for performance adequacy shall be approved by the contracting officer.

name: Acceptable to Compress Duty Cycle for Low Duty Assemblies
text: For items having a relatively low percentage duty cycle, it shall be acceptable to compress the operational cycle into a tolerable total test duration.

name: Accelerated Testing Allowed for High Duty Assemblies
text: For assemblies which operate continuously on-orbit, or at very high percentage duty cycles, accelerated test techniques may be employed if such an approach can be shown to be valid.

name: Environmental Testing with Disassembly and Inspection
text: For such assemblies, tests shall be under proper environmental conditions, of varying duration, followed by disassembly and inspection.

name: Test Items Must Be Essentially Identical to Flight Items
text: The moving mechanical assemblies used for life test shall be essentially identical with the flight items.

name: Instrumentation Differences Must Not Jeopardize Validity
text: The only differences between the test items and the flight items shall be those changes necessary for incorporation of test instrumentation. These differences shall not jeopardize the validity of the tests with respect to the flight hardware.

name: Instrumentation Required to Monitor Operating Conditions
text: Sufficient instrumentation shall be used to provide knowledge of the operating conditions within the assemblies during life testing such as internal pressures, temperatures, and temperature gradients.

name: Periodic Disassembly and Inspection During Life Testing
text: A single item may be used provided that periodic disassembly and inspection are performed to determine wear, lubrication condition, and any degradation that may affect performance.
